examples.title: Thermometer Test
examples.content: True water boiling point: 100°C. Thermometer A reads 100.1°C (very close = accurate). Thermometer B reads 98°C (still close = somewhat accurate). Thermometer C reads 110°C (way off = NOT accurate).

examples.title: Weight Scale Accuracy
examples.content: Your actual weight: 65 kg (measured on a medical scale). Your home scale says 65.2 kg. Accurate! Your old scale says 62 kg. Not so accurate (3 kg off).

examples.title: Book Length Measurement
examples.content: A book is actually 20 cm long (you verified with a calibrated ruler). Your ruler says 19.9 cm. Very accurate! Your meter stick says 18 cm. Not accurate.
